name: Gargoyle Sorceress
tips: 20🗡️/50🤍 for 5 mana is already a wall. Placement matters — drop her one row in front of a Dark ranged ally and the Dark Matter buff triples.
Best pair: a Dark ranged minion (Range > 0, Element Dark) sitting behind her in the same column — Shadow Blaster and Grave Caller both qualify. Erebus does not (Range 0, melee).
The buff reads your Dark Matter POOL — a player resource. Without the placement condition you get +pool🗡️/+pool🤍. With it you get +3×pool🗡️/+3×pool🤍. At 5 Dark Matter that's +15🗡️/+15🤍 vs +5🗡️/+5🤍.
She's a Dark Mage herself — once on board she counts toward every per-Dark-Mage gain and receives Dark Matter Stash's buff.

Discarding Dark Matter Stash to pay her cost triggers its on-Discard gain (+1 Dark Matter per Dark Mage already on the board). That gain lands BEFORE her Summon buff resolves, so it pumps her own buff.
rulings: The placement condition checks the tile directly behind her (one row toward your own back row) when her Summon effects resolve — after the post-summon react window. A react that removes the Dark ranged ally behind her before then cancels the triple; a friendly Dark ranged minion must sit there at resolution, i.e. she must be directly in front of it.
condition_multiplier applies to the scaled amount: base 0 + YOUR Dark Matter pool = N, then N × 3 = final buff. Dark Matter is a player pool (redesign 2026-07) — she carries no stacks of her own.
Both buffs (atk and hp) share the same placement condition — either both triple or neither triples.

She is a [[Dark Mage]] (element Dark, tribe Mage) — she counts toward per-Dark-Mage gains and receives Dark-Mage-targeted buffs once on board. She is NOT on the board yet while her own summon costs are being paid, so a cost-discard's gain does not count her.
Discarded cards go to the Exhaust pile, not the Graveyard. Cost-discards fire on-Discard triggers (e.g. Dark Matter Stash's Dark Matter gain).
